linkPolicy: Only real public URLs are included. Unavailable project destinations use a null href and an intentional status.
visualArtifactPolicy: Live projects may include real screenshot artifacts. Projects without uploaded screenshots use intentional artifact status cards instead of fake images.

projects.name: PermitPulse
projects.currentStatus: Deployed on secure Cloudflare infrastructure with authenticated operations, private evidence storage, mobile access, and deterministic HTML/PDF packet rendering.
projects.summary: An engineering expedition into fragmented public records: source evidence is preserved, chronology is reconstructed, human review stays explicit, and approved case material becomes a deterministic client packet.
projects.whatItProves: Uneven permit records can be carried through one traceable workflow—from source capture and provenance to reviewer-controlled, professional deliverables—without hiding uncertainty.
projects.whatBroke: Jurisdiction records arrived with inconsistent structure, incomplete ownership signals, stale portal states, and chronology that had to be reconstructed rather than assumed.
projects.humanFlaws: The system keeps open conditions, source gaps, reviewer judgment, and unresolved agency handoffs visible instead of manufacturing certainty.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: AI accelerated research organization, implementation passes, interface iteration, and test coverage. Evidence approval, interpretation, and delivery decisions remain human-controlled.
projects.nextUpgrade: Continue hardening jurisdiction-specific source assumptions, reviewer safeguards, and packet validation as new record conditions appear.

engineeringNotes.summary: React and TypeScript shape the operational interface. Cloudflare Workers run the application edge, D1 holds structured case state, R2 keeps evidence private, and Better Auth protects the workspace. The packet engine produces consistent HTML and PDF deliverables from reviewed records.

projects.name: Xibalba Pinball
projects.summary: A playable mythic arcade experiment with underworld atmosphere, browser physics, and mobile-first game feel.
projects.whatItProves: AI can help ship weird, playable, visually distinctive browser games before the design is perfect.
projects.whatBroke: Table tuning fought the art direction, collisions misbehaved, and several visual passes missed the intended underworld mood.
projects.humanFlaws: Imperfect tuning, collision edge cases, mobile control pressure, and taste decisions that had to be earned through failed passes.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Physics implementation support, Phaser iteration, asset direction prompts, debugging loops, and mobile polish.
projects.nextUpgrade: Tighten table physics, add a clearer scoring loop, and turn the best collision surprises into deliberate mechanics.

projects.name: Danger Close
projects.summary: A tactical browser survival game about piloting a salvage drone through dead zones, collecting resources, surviving enemy pressure, and upgrading hardware between runs.
projects.whatItProves: AI-assisted development can ship a polished browser game loop with sector selection, upgrade systems, enemy pressure, resource collection, survival pacing, and premium sci-fi interface design.
projects.whatBroke: Early balancing passes created uneven pressure, dense combat states reduced clarity, and experimental systems did not always share a clean plan.
projects.humanFlaws: Balancing is still evolving, visual density needs tuning, and some systems grew from rough experiments rather than a perfect plan.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Used Codex and frontier AI models for gameplay systems, UI polish, enemy behavior, upgrade design, copy, testing checklists, and rapid iteration.
projects.nextUpgrade: More enemy variety, better map drama, stronger audio/impact polish, and a sharper onboarding flow.

visualArtifact.alt: Danger Close browser survival game screenshot

additionalVisualArtifacts.alt: Danger Close combat in the Dead Datacenter screenshot
additionalVisualArtifacts.label: Combat / Dead Datacenter

projects.name: BounceBox
projects.subtitle: Physics Groovebox
projects.summary: A mobile-first physics groovebox that turns bouncing balls and AI-generated MIDI patterns into a playable instrument.
projects.whatItProves: Browser-native physics, audio, generative controls, and mobile UI can become a flagship playable instrument.
projects.whatBroke: Rhythm feel, touch ergonomics, MIDI prompt boundaries, and dense performance controls all had to be tuned into something playable.
projects.humanFlaws: Ambitious scope, strange interaction design, performance-control density, and the taste work of making weird mechanics feel intentional.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Custom ChatGPT MIDI Lab, generative pattern design, TypeScript iteration, mobile UI passes, control naming, and performance-state testing.
projects.nextUpgrade: Deepen the MIDI Lab, refine groove snapshot recall, and document the performance workflow as a public case study.

projects.name: Black Swan
projects.summary: A survival-loop prototype with travel, mining, events, upgrades, and physics-driven risk.
projects.whatItProves: A rough game premise can become a functioning loop with movement, resources, upgrades, event pressure, and progression.
projects.whatBroke: Pacing was uneven, spawn logic created strange difficulty spikes, and balancing exposed how much design judgment still matters.
projects.humanFlaws: Ambitious scope, balancing unknowns, testing blind spots, and systems that needed more constraint than the first idea allowed.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Game-system scaffolding, debugging prompts, staged roadmap planning, event design, and test-case generation.
projects.nextUpgrade: Improve event pacing, economy pressure, save-state clarity, and risk/reward choices that make each run legible.

projects.name: Pumpkin AR Face Filter
projects.summary: A playful camera experiment shaped around face tracking, seasonal overlays, and kid-tested reactions.
projects.whatItProves: AI-assisted prototyping can produce playful camera experiences that respond to real feedback, not just a spec.
projects.whatBroke: Face tracking drifted, assets needed repeated alignment, and spawned effects needed more restraint than the first pass had.
projects.humanFlaws: Tracking imperfections, asset alignment drift, mouth-spawn tuning, and the unpredictable standard of kid-tested delight.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Camera UI support, asset-prompt iteration, interaction logic, edge-case debugging, and quick feedback loops.
projects.nextUpgrade: Stabilize tracking, tune spawned effects, improve capture/share flow, and package the toy as a cleaner mobile demo.

projects.name: Snapshot Studio
projects.category: Local business / AI search audit tool
projects.summary: A lightweight sales tool for turning local visibility problems into fast visual reports and improvement plans.
projects.whatItProves: Small businesses can receive fast, visual snapshots of search visibility problems and practical improvement plans.
projects.whatBroke: The offer started too broad, outreach responses were inconsistent, and report value had to be made obvious faster.
projects.humanFlaws: Positioning uncertainty, lead-response friction, messy prospect context, and the gap between a useful audit and a paid offer.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Prospect research, report drafting, local search framing, landing-page passes, and repeatable audit structure.
projects.nextUpgrade: Tighten the offer, standardize report templates, reduce prospect friction, and add clearer before/after examples.

projects.name: The Angeles Crest
projects.summary: A public-facing brand for packaging design, automation, local services, and AI execution into a premium web presence.
projects.whatItProves: Sergio can package taste, automation, service design, local business sense, and AI execution into a credible public brand.
projects.whatBroke: The offer evolved in public, the strongest wedge was not obvious at first, and the brand had to absorb several directions.
projects.humanFlaws: Offer changes, messy evolution, ambition outrunning the first package, and the ongoing work of choosing the sharpest wedge.
projects.aiLeverageUsed: Copy exploration, site design, service packaging, visual direction, deployment help, and faster positioning passes.
projects.nextUpgrade: Connect the strongest proofs to a more direct service path and publish the right case-study routes when the brand page is ready.
